strengths: Three objects are clearly identified with specific real-world contexts (publication details, speaker, family home).
Links to the IA prompt are explicit and supported by relevant TOK concepts (tacit vs explicit knowledge, framing, causal claims).
Justifications are well-supported by scholarly references and diverse sources (ResearchGate, reputable publishers, podcast, dictionary).
Thoughtful synthesis shown in extending examples (e.g., superstition, political rhetoric) to reinforce the theme of personal experience shaping knowledge.
Integration of illustrative and empirical evidence (Maryâs Room, YouTube lecture, feng-shui statues) demonstrates depth and real-world grounding.
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent or incomplete (missing uploaderâs name, italics, retrieval dates).
Depth of analysis varies between objects, with some descriptive digressions interrupting focus on the prompt.
Minor lapses in TOK terminology (e.g., framing not always explicitly named, correlation vs causation not sharply distinguished).
Lacks a concise conclusion or captions to unify the three objects under the prompt.
Use of a thought-experiment illustration (Maryâs Room) falls slightly outside the expectation of a naturally occurring artefact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets all the descriptors for the 7â8 band. Objects are grounded in clear real-world contexts and linked convincingly to the prompt through appropriate TOK terminology and evidence. Scholarly references demonstrate academic rigour and thoughtful sourcing. Minor inconsistencies in citation formatting, uneven analytical depth, and occasional descriptive tangents prevent elevation to the 9â10 band. Overall, this submission justifies a band-8 score, reflecting strong performance with room for minor refinements.

annotations.comment: The student clearly situates Maryâs Room in its original publication context, demonstrating strong grounding in a real-world artefact and its philosophical origin.

Although the illustration is compelling, note that a thought experiment graphic is not a naturally occurring artefact. The student could reflect on how this choice affects the authenticity of the real-world link.
The synesthesia and mantis shrimp examples effectively illustrate the limits of explicit versus tacit knowledge, enriching the analysis of sensory experience.
The bike-riding illustration is interesting but could be more concise. Tighten this example and directly tie it back to âpersonal experience and knowledgeâ to maintain focus.
The distinction between explicit and tacit knowledge is explicit, yet the paragraph would benefit from a stronger tie back to how Maryâs personal experience changes her knowledge.
Consider ending Object 1 with a sentence explicitly referencing the prompt phrase âpersonal experience and knowledgeâ to solidify the connection.
The student provides precise details (date, presenter, title) for Object 2, demonstrating a clear real-world grounding in the chosen YouTube lecture.
Clarify how selecting a public YouTube video satisfies the artefact specificity requirement, perhaps by noting unique aspects of this recording.
Strong integration of framing theory, with APA and Thon & Jucks sources, underpins the explanation of how presenters shape audience knowledge.
The exploration of childhood obedience introduces an interesting psychological angle but diverges from the main prompt; consider trimming this for coherence.
The student could deepen the TOK analysis by explicitly naming âframingâ as a way personal experience shapes perceived knowledge.
Introduce a TOK concept such as âauthority biasâ to enrich the analysis of why audiences accept faulty claims based on presenter credibility.
The political rhetoric bridge is a thoughtful insight but interrupts the object structure; consider relocating it to a formal introduction or conclusion.
Object 3 is grounded in a personal photograph and family context, satisfying the requirement for specific real-world artefacts.
The use of Hinterthuerâs discussion on illusory correlation effectively conveys why personal experiences can override conflicting scientific knowledge.
Differentiate more clearly between correlation and causation in the Feng Shui discussion to sharpen the TOK focus on causal knowledge.
To balance subjective family experience, the student could cite academic studies on Feng Shui beliefs or cultural cognition.
End the Object 3 section with a direct reference to how these experiences illustrate the promptâs theme of personal experience shaping knowledge.
The reference to superstitions (knocking on wood) creatively extends the theme beyond the objects, showing thoughtful synthesis.
A concise conclusion section summarizing insights from all three objects would strengthen the overall cohesion of the exhibition.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics, incomplete retrieval dates); this undermines academic rigor in the exhibition.
Standardize all entries (titles, authors, italics) and include complete retrieval dates in APA format to enhance credibility.
Including diverse reference types (dictionary, podcast, research articles) reflects thorough sourcing and supports multiple perspectives.
Verify each URL and eliminate line breaks in web addresses to ensure references lead directly to the source.
The YouTube entry lacks the uploaderâs name and video format; complete the citation with full APA details for video sources.
Include the channel or publisherâs full name and specify the medium when citing videos to adhere to APA guidelines.
The inclusion of a ResearchGate article and reputable publishers demonstrates diligent sourcing beyond simple online content.
Consider adding brief captions beneath each object image to guide the viewerâs interpretation of its relationship to the prompt.
Separate long blocks of reference text into distinct entries for readability and easier verification of sources.
How might the exhibition be enriched by adding a fourth object that presents a counterargument to the relationship between experience and knowledge?

strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
